mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in over 65% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when RSAFS and HOUST both rise, with out-of-sample accuracy below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to correlate with positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX returns remain positive in 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.4%, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and UNRATE signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate revenue bases and profit margins, creating a persistent upward drift in S&P 500 prices over the following quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 60-day SPX direction, with a hit rate indistinguishable from random chance (50% +/- 2%).

mechanism_narrative: A breach of the six-month industrial production moving average signals a supply-side contraction that disrupts corporate supply chains and earnings forecasts, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in over 60% of instances where INDPRO drops below its 6-month trend.
